understanding.function: Nuclear fuel reprocessing system for extracting fissile material (uranium and plutonium) from spent nuclear fuel waste, capable of processing multiple fuel types (UOX, MOX, ERU/REU) with 1800 tHM/year capacity
understanding.domain: Nuclear fuel cycle - spent fuel reprocessing and fissile material recovery
constraints: Product purity standards for downstream fuel fabrication
uncertainties: Specific technical processes and equipment details not defined
Exact safety and regulatory requirements beyond ALARA principle
Interface specifications with upstream waste reception and downstream fuel fabrication systems
Detailed operational parameters and control setpoints
Emergency response and accident scenarios not described

candidates.name: PUREX Process
candidates.description: Plutonium Uranium Redox EXtraction process using tributyl phosphate (TBP) solvent extraction for separating uranium and plutonium from spent nuclear fuel
candidates.function_alignment: Direct match for uranium and plutonium extraction from spent fuel with established industrial scale capability
candidates.notes: Most widely used commercial reprocessing technology, proven for UOX and MOX fuel types

candidates.name: Hydrometallurgical Reprocessing
candidates.description: Aqueous-based chemical processing for reprocessing mixed uranium-plutonium nuclear fuel with short cooling times
candidates.function_alignment: Suitable for multiple fuel types including mixed U-Pu fuels, demonstrated for fast reactor fuels
candidates.notes: Technology for BREST reactor fuel, capable of processing fuel with less than 2 years cooling time

candidates.name: Spent Fuel Dissolution and Reprocessing Processes
candidates.description: Comprehensive fuel dissolution and chemical separation processes for spent nuclear fuel treatment
candidates.function_alignment: Essential front-end process for any reprocessing system handling multiple fuel types
candidates.notes: Covers dissolution chemistry and initial processing steps for various fuel matrices

candidates.name: COEX Process
candidates.description: Co-extraction process for simultaneous recovery of uranium and plutonium without pure plutonium separation
candidates.domain: Nuclear fuel reprocessing - advanced solvent extraction
candidates.function_alignment: Advanced reprocessing technology with enhanced proliferation resistance while maintaining fissile material recovery
candidates.notes: Developed as proliferation-resistant alternative to PUREX, suitable for industrial scale

candidates.name: GANEX Process
candidates.description: Grouped ActiNide EXtraction process for simultaneous extraction of uranium, plutonium, and minor actinides
candidates.function_alignment: Advanced process capable of recovering fissile materials while addressing minor actinide management
candidates.notes: Next-generation technology for comprehensive actinide recovery from spent fuel

candidates.name: Remote Handling Systems for Nuclear Reprocessing
candidates.description: Automated and remotely operated equipment systems for handling radioactive materials in reprocessing facilities
candidates.function_alignment: Essential for ALARA compliance and operational availability in high-radiation reprocessing environment
candidates.notes: Critical technology for maintaining operations while minimizing radiation exposure

candidates.name: Tributyl Phosphate (TBP) Solvent Extraction
candidates.description: Chemical solvent extraction technology using TBP in hydrocarbon diluent for actinide separation
candidates.function_alignment: Core separation technology for uranium and plutonium recovery with proven industrial scalability
candidates.notes: Fundamental chemistry basis for PUREX and related processes, well-established for large-scale operations

candidates.name: Nuclear Fuel Reprocessing Plant Process Control Systems
candidates.description: Automated process control and monitoring systems for nuclear fuel reprocessing operations
candidates.function_alignment: Essential for maintaining product purity standards and operational availability requirements
candidates.notes: Critical for consistent operation and quality control in high-capacity reprocessing facilities

candidates.name: Multi-stage Centrifugal Contactors
candidates.description: High-efficiency liquid-liquid extraction equipment for solvent extraction processes in nuclear reprocessing
candidates.function_alignment: High-throughput separation equipment suitable for 1800 tHM/year capacity requirements
candidates.notes: Proven technology for industrial-scale solvent extraction with compact footprint and high efficiency

candidates.name: Hot Cell Technology
candidates.description: Heavily shielded containment systems for handling highly radioactive materials with remote manipulation capabilities
candidates.function_alignment: Essential containment technology for ALARA compliance and radiological safety during reprocessing operations
candidates.notes: Fundamental infrastructure for safe handling of spent fuel and radioactive process streams

candidates.name: PUREX (Plutonium Uranium Redox Extraction) Process
candidates.description: Established aqueous reprocessing technology using tributyl phosphate (TBP) in kerosene to separate uranium and plutonium from fission products through solvent extraction. Widely implemented at industrial scale including La Hague facility.
candidates.function_alignment: Direct - primary industrial method for uranium/plutonium extraction from spent fuel with proven multi-fuel type capability
candidates.notes: Most mature technology for large-scale reprocessing, forms basis for most commercial facilities

candidates.name: COEX (Co-Extraction) Process
candidates.description: Modified PUREX process that co-extracts uranium and plutonium together rather than separately, designed to reduce proliferation risks while maintaining reprocessing efficiency for fuel cycle applications.
candidates.function_alignment: High - addresses same function with enhanced proliferation resistance, suitable for MOX and multiple fuel types
candidates.notes: Developed by CEA/AREVA as evolution of PUREX with improved safeguards characteristics

candidates.name: Hot Cell Remote Handling Systems
candidates.description: Heavily shielded containment facilities with remote manipulation equipment for handling highly radioactive materials, essential infrastructure for spent fuel reprocessing operations and maintenance.
candidates.function_alignment: High - essential supporting technology for safe operation of reprocessing systems under ALARA principles
candidates.notes: Critical for maintaining radiological safety during processing and maintenance operations

candidates.name: Pyroprocessing/Electrorefining
candidates.description: High-temperature molten salt electrochemical process for separating actinides from fission products, offering compact footprint and inherent proliferation resistance through inability to separate pure plutonium.
candidates.function_alignment: Medium - alternative technology for fissile material recovery with different product characteristics
candidates.notes: Produces mixed actinide products rather than separated U/Pu, may not meet purity requirements

candidates.name: Nuclear Material Accountancy and Control Systems
candidates.description: Integrated measurement and monitoring systems for tracking nuclear material quantities and compositions throughout reprocessing operations, essential for safeguards compliance and process control.
candidates.function_alignment: High - mandatory supporting system for regulatory compliance and operational control of fissile material processing
candidates.notes: Required for French/EURATOM regulatory compliance and international safeguards

candidates.name: Vitrification Systems for High-Level Waste
candidates.description: Glass matrix immobilization technology for high-level radioactive waste streams from reprocessing, converting liquid waste into stable glass forms for long-term storage and disposal.
candidates.function_alignment: Medium - handles waste streams from reprocessing operations, essential for complete facility design
candidates.notes: Necessary for managing non-recoverable fission product waste from separation processes

enriched.name: PUREX Process
enriched.description: Plutonium Uranium Redox EXtraction process using tributyl phosphate (TBP) solvent extraction for separating uranium and plutonium from spent nuclear fuel
enriched.function_alignment: Direct match for uranium and plutonium extraction from spent fuel with established industrial scale capability
enriched.notes: Most mature and widely deployed reprocessing technology, currently operating at La Hague at similar capacity

enriched.gap_analysis: Perfect alignment - proven industrial technology for uranium/plutonium extraction from UOX, MOX, and ERU fuels at required capacity scale.

enriched.name: Hydrometallurgical Reprocessing
enriched.description: Aqueous-based chemical processing for reprocessing mixed uranium-plutonium nuclear fuel with short cooling times
enriched.function_alignment: Suitable for multiple fuel types including mixed U-Pu fuels, demonstrated for fast reactor fuels
enriched.notes: Proven technology but less mature than PUREX for large-scale operations, suitable for diverse fuel matrices
enriched.gap_analysis: Good alignment for multiple fuel types including mixed U-Pu fuels. Demonstrated for fast reactor fuels but limited large-scale commercial deployment.

enriched.name: Spent Fuel Dissolution and Reprocessing Processes
enriched.description: Comprehensive fuel dissolution and chemical separation processes for spent nuclear fuel treatment
enriched.function_alignment: Essential front-end process for any reprocessing system handling multiple fuel types
enriched.notes: Fundamental technology required for all reprocessing approaches, well-established for various fuel matrices

enriched.name: COEX Process
enriched.description: Co-extraction process for simultaneous recovery of uranium and plutonium without pure plutonium separation
enriched.domain: Nuclear fuel reprocessing - advanced solvent extraction
enriched.function_alignment: Advanced reprocessing technology with enhanced proliferation resistance while maintaining fissile material recovery
enriched.notes: Advanced evolution of PUREX with improved safeguards, suitable for industrial scale but less commercially proven
enriched.gap_analysis: Strong alignment with enhanced proliferation resistance. Achieves fissile material recovery function while addressing regulatory concerns.

enriched.name: GANEX Process
enriched.description: Grouped ActiNide EXtraction process for simultaneous extraction of uranium, plutonium, and minor actinides
enriched.function_alignment: Advanced process capable of recovering fissile materials while addressing minor actinide management
enriched.notes: Next-generation technology with broader scope than required, but lower maturity for near-term implementation

enriched.name: Hot Cell Technology
enriched.description: Heavily shielded containment systems for handling highly radioactive materials with remote manipulation capabilities
enriched.function_alignment: Essential containment technology for ALARA compliance and radiological safety during reprocessing operations
enriched.notes: Fundamental and mature infrastructure technology for safe handling of highly radioactive materials
enriched.gap_analysis: Essential containment technology for ALARA compliance and radiological safety. Required infrastructure but not core separation function.

enriched.name: PUREX (Plutonium Uranium Redox Extraction) Process
enriched.description: Established aqueous reprocessing technology using tributyl phosphate (TBP) in kerosene to separate uranium and plutonium from fission products through solvent extraction. Widely implemented at industrial scale including La Hague facility.
enriched.function_alignment: Direct - primary industrial method for uranium/plutonium extraction from spent fuel with proven multi-fuel type capability
enriched.notes: Duplicate entry of most proven technology, currently operating at required scale at La Hague
enriched.gap_analysis: Perfect alignment - most mature industrial method for uranium/plutonium extraction with proven capability for all required fuel types.

enriched.name: COEX (Co-Extraction) Process
enriched.description: Modified PUREX process that co-extracts uranium and plutonium together rather than separately, designed to reduce proliferation risks while maintaining reprocessing efficiency for fuel cycle applications.
enriched.function_alignment: High - addresses same function with enhanced proliferation resistance, suitable for MOX and multiple fuel types
enriched.notes: Duplicate entry - CEA-developed evolution of PUREX with enhanced safeguards but less commercial deployment
enriched.gap_analysis: Strong alignment with enhanced proliferation resistance. Achieves same function as PUREX with improved safeguards characteristics.
